items.headline: Game 7! Spurs Force Decisive Western Conference Finals Showdown!
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs delivered a dominant performance in Game 6, routing the Oklahoma City Thunder 118-91 to force a winner-take-all Game 7 in the Western Conference Finals. Victor Wembanyama led the charge for the Spurs.

items.headline: NBA Board of Governors Approves Sweeping Anti-Tanking Draft Lottery Changes
items.summary: In a significant league announcement, the NBA's Board of Governors officially passed new anti-tanking measures for the draft lottery. The changes, effective for the next three seasons, expand the lottery to 16 teams and flatten the odds for the No. 1 pick, aiming to discourage teams from intentionally losing games.

items.headline: Spurs Dominate Thunder in Game 6 to Force Decider
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs kept their NBA Finals hopes alive with a resounding victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals. The series now heads back to Oklahoma City for a thrilling Game 7.

items.headline: Wemby Forces Game 7 with Dominant Performance
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama put on a show in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals, leading the San Antonio Spurs to victory. He finished with 28 points, 10 rebounds, and 3 blocks in just 28 minutes, setting the tone early with two 3-pointers and a block in the game's opening moments.

items.headline: Rookie Harper Provides Crucial Spark Off Bench
items.summary: Spurs rookie guard Dylan Harper stepped up in a big way during Game 6, contributing 18 points off the bench and attacking aggressively to ignite San Antonio's offense when needed.

items.headline: MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Held in Check
items.summary: Reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had a tough outing in Game 6, limited to a season-low 15 points on 6-for-18 shooting. This marks his lowest scoring performance since the 2025 playoffs.

items.headline: Stephon Castle's Playmaking on Display
items.summary: Stephon Castle made significant contributions for the Spurs in Game 6, not only scoring 17 points but also dishing out an impressive 9 assists.

items.headline: New York Knicks Punch Ticket to NBA Finals
items.summary: The New York Knicks have secured their spot in the NBA Finals after a dominant 4-0 sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals. They now await the winner of the Western Conference showdown.

items.headline: Milan Momcilovic Withdraws from 2026 NBA Draft
items.summary: Former Iowa State standout Milan Momcilovic has opted to withdraw from the 2026 NBA Draft and will return to college basketball for his senior season.

items.headline: No NBA Games Scheduled for May 29th
items.summary: Following last night's thrilling Game 6 in the Western Conference Finals, there are no NBA games scheduled for today, May 29, 2026. Action will resume with Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals on Saturday.
